_rationale: code_insight mode=structure produces a LCF section listing directory/file/symbol counts and file-extension distribution. After iter-5 fix (task AQQfI8XEEkV6: src/cli/mcp.cpp register_parity_compat_tools now branches on arguments.mode), C++ emits the per-mode LCF payload — same dirs/files/symbols/depth/categories/top_dirs lines as Go. NEW GO BUG SURFACED iter-7: Go's `types:` line iterates a `map[string]int` of file-extension counts in randomised order (Go runtime map-iteration is intentionally non-deterministic). 10/10 stability check: 2 PASS / 8 FAIL — every failure is exclusively a permutation of the `types: .go=1 .py=1 .rs=1 .cpp=1` line. C++ emits these in deterministic (insertion or sorted) order. Two viable fixes upstream: (a) Go emits `types:` sorted by extension; (b) C++ replicates Go's randomisation (rejected — karpathy-rule-6: introducing non-determinism to match a bug is a fallback). Until Go is fixed, text body cannot be in stable tier — locked to envelope-only here. The 4 other code_insight mode descriptors (statistics/unified/git_analyze/git_hotspots) all hold text-stable because their payloads have no map-iteration-ordered sections. Probe: /tmp/parity-iter4/code_insight___mode___structure.{go,cpp}.json — sizes match (290 vs 290) confirming structural parity; only line-internal ordering diverges.

tiers._rationale_stable: Envelope only. Locks dispatch + content[].type='text' frame. Tighter text-body parity blocked on Go-side map-iteration nondeterminism on the `types:` line. When Go sorts the types line (filed as iter-7 fix subtask), flip text to stable.
tiers._rationale_ignore: result.content[].text varies run-to-run on the Go side because the `types:` line iterates a Go map in randomised order. NOT a silent fallback — bug filed against Go; descriptor will tighten to stable text once Go emits sorted types.
